name: QuestBridge College Prep Scholars Program
description: QuestBridge College Prep Scholars connects high-achieving juniors from low-income backgrounds with college admissions and financial aid resources. Scholars gain access to campus visits, mentoring, and college application fee waivers.
eligibility: High school juniors from low-income households with strong academic records (typically top 5-10% of class)

name: MITES Semester (formerly MOSTEC)
description: MIT Introduction to Technology, Engineering, and Science (MITES) Semester is a six-month online program for rising seniors from underrepresented backgrounds. Students engage in rigorous STEM coursework, mentoring, and community building with MIT faculty and students.
eligibility: Rising high school seniors from underrepresented or underserved backgrounds with strong interest in STEM

name: Stanford Pre-Collegiate Summer Institutes
description: Stanford Pre-Collegiate Summer Institutes offer intensive academic experiences for intellectually curious high school students. Students live on the Stanford campus and take deep-dive courses in subjects ranging from astrophysics to creative writing.
eligibility: High school students in grades 9-11 with strong academic records and intellectual curiosity

name: American Legion Colorado Boys State
description: Colorado Boys State is a week-long immersive civic education program where rising seniors learn about government by creating and running a mock state. Delegates campaign for offices, pass legislation, and experience democracy firsthand.
eligibility: Rising high school seniors (boys) in Colorado, nominated by their local American Legion post or high school counselor
cost: Free (sponsored by local American Legion posts)

name: American Legion Auxiliary Colorado Girls State
description: Colorado Girls State is a week-long leadership and civic education program for young women. Participants learn about government operations, run for mock offices, and develop leadership skills in a hands-on political simulation.
eligibility: Rising high school seniors (girls) in Colorado, nominated by their school or local American Legion Auxiliary unit

name: CU Boulder Pre-Collegiate Program
description: CU Boulder's Pre-Collegiate Program allows high school students to take university-level courses for college credit during the summer. Students experience campus life while earning transferable credits and exploring academic interests.
eligibility: High school students in grades 10-12 with a minimum 3.0 GPA

name: Lorenzo de Zavala Youth Legislative Session
description: The LYL (Lorenzo de Zavala Youth Legislative Session) is a five-day simulation of the Texas Legislature designed for Latino high school juniors and seniors. Participants draft bills, debate policy, and develop leadership and public speaking skills.
eligibility: Hispanic/Latino high school juniors and seniors with interest in public policy and leadership

name: NASA STEM High School Internship
description: NASA offers summer internships for high school students at various NASA centers across the country. Interns work alongside NASA scientists and engineers on real projects, gaining hands-on experience in aerospace, engineering, and research.
eligibility: U.S. citizen high school students age 16+, with a minimum 3.0 GPA and strong interest in STEM

name: Colorado Upward Bound
organization: U.S. Department of Education / Colorado Colleges
description: Upward Bound provides academic instruction, tutoring, counseling, and mentoring to first-generation and low-income high school students. The program runs year-round with an intensive summer residential component on a college campus.
eligibility: First-generation college students and/or low-income high school students in grades 9-12
duration: Multi-year (9th grade through high school graduation)

name: Girls Who Code Summer Immersion Program
description: The Girls Who Code Summer Immersion Program is a free two-week virtual program where rising juniors and seniors learn computer science, gain exposure to tech jobs, and join a supportive community of peers. Participants build real projects and connect with industry mentors.
eligibility: Rising junior or senior girls and non-binary students with interest in computer science (no prior experience required)

name: Bank of America Student Leaders Program
description: The Student Leaders Program connects community-minded high school juniors and seniors with eight-week paid internships at local nonprofits. Participants also attend the Student Leaders Summit in Washington, D.C., gaining leadership and career development skills.
eligibility: High school juniors and seniors with strong community involvement and leadership

name: Colorado School of Mines Summer Engineering Camp
description: Colorado School of Mines offers week-long summer camps for high school students interested in engineering, computer science, and the applied sciences. Campers participate in hands-on labs, field trips, and project-based learning with Mines faculty.
eligibility: High school students in grades 9-12 interested in engineering and STEM fields

name: Summer Search Colorado
description: Summer Search is a national youth development organization that provides low-income high school students with transformative outdoor experiences, mentoring, and college and career support. The multi-year program begins sophomore year and continues through college.
eligibility: Low-income high school sophomores in the Denver metro area nominated by a teacher or counselor

name: Carnegie Mellon Pre-College Programs
description: Carnegie Mellon's pre-college programs offer intensive summer experiences in areas including computer science, art, drama, music, and architecture. Students earn college credit while experiencing the academic rigor of a top-ranked university.
eligibility: High school students in grades 10-12 with strong academic records

name: Denver Youth Congress
description: Denver Youth Congress empowers young people to be civically engaged by placing them in advisory roles to Denver city government. Members work on policy issues affecting youth, develop advocacy skills, and advise elected officials.
eligibility: Denver residents in grades 9-12 with interest in civic engagement and community leadership
